decks.notes: The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful combat tricks. Utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game. Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer provide card advantage and board presence, while Goblin Bombardment adds an extra layer of reach to finish off the opponent. The deck also includes utility cards like Thraben Charm and Voice of Victory to help control the board and protect key creatures.

This deck focuses on quickly putting powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Fallaji Archaeologist onto the battlefield using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. It also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect the combo. Otherworldly Gaze and Meticulous Archive help to filter through the deck and find key pieces. The goal is to reanimate a big threat and win the game in one explosive turn, overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats and instant-speed reanimation spells.

The deck focuses on disrupting and controlling the opponent's resources while using land destruction and removal spells to gain advantage. It aims to slow down the opponent's game plan, deny them key resources, and eventually overwhelm them with powerful creatures and spells that can control the board. By utilizing a mix of land destruction, removal, and powerful creatures, the deck aims to take control of the game and dictate the pace to secure victory.

The main strategy of this deck revolves around quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. It also utilizes instant spells like Solitude and Prismatic Ending for removal and protection, while also including disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to control the game. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, unexpected plays that can win the game in a single turn.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to quickly build a board presence and apply pressure on the opponent. It includes cards that allow for artifact recursion and card advantage, along with a mix of disruptive spells and efficient creatures to control the game. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with an aggressive strategy that can quickly end the game before the opponent can stabilize. The synergy between artifacts and spells creates a fast-paced and explosive playstyle that can catch opponents off guard.

The main concept of this Ruby Storm deck is to use cards like Ruby Medallion and Pyretic Ritual to generate mana quickly, allowing you to cast powerful spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Grapeshot to combo off and deal massive damage to your opponent. Past in Flames and Flashback help you recur spells from your graveyard, while cards like Wish and Elegant Parlor provide additional versatility and card advantage. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through countering spells, removal, and powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler. It also utilizes Isochron Scepter to create value from repetitive spells like Orim's Chant and Counterspell. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan, protect its own threats, and ultimately win the game through incremental advantage and resource management. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion provides additional synergy and a potential creature threat to complement the control strategy.

The deck focuses on fast-paced aggression and card advantage through cards like Mishra's Bauble, Slickshot Show-Off, and Dragon's Rage Channeler. By utilizing cards that provide card selection and cantrips, the deck aims to maintain a high level of consistency and pressure on the opponent. With a low mana curve and efficient removal in Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, the deck can quickly close out games by overwhelming the opponent with threats while also disrupting their game plan.

The key concept of this deck is to use aggressive creatures to apply pressure on the opponent while also utilizing powerful blinking effects to protect and reuse key creatures. By blinking creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Starfield Shepherd with cards like Ephemerate and Haliya, Guided by Light, players can maintain board presence and generate value from their creatures. This deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of aggro tactics and strategic blinking to control the battlefield and secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly playing creatures with powerful enter the battlefield abilities and using blink effects to abuse those abilities multiple times. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggro pressure and value generation through blinking creatures like Quantum Riddler and Guide of Souls. Utilizing cards like Ephemerate and March of Otherworldly Light, the deck can protect its creatures and create advantageous board states. The key strategy is to outvalue the opponent while maintaining a high tempo to close out games swiftly.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana using Urza's lands to quickly cast powerful Eldrazi creatures and control spells. The key strategy is to disrupt opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Grafdigger's Cage while deploying threats like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms to dominate the battlefield. The variety of removal and disruption spells combined with the huge Eldrazi creatures make it difficult for opponents to keep up, ultimately leading to victory through overwhelming board presence and resource advantage.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and sacrificing them for various effects to overwhelm the opponent quickly. By using cards like Arcbound Ravager and Claws of Gix, along with synergistic cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga, the deck aims to create a fast-paced, aggressive strategy. The inclusion of cards like Tormod's Crypt and Engineered Explosives helps to control the opponent's graveyard and board state respectively. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Metallic Rebuke provide additional utility and disruption, making the deck versatile and adaptable to different matchups.

This Modern control deck utilizes the UrzaTron lands to quickly ramp into powerful threats like Karn, the Great Creator, Ugin, the Spirit Dragon, and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger. It also includes disruptive creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Sire of Seven Deaths to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to control the game through a combination of counter spells, removal, and large threats, overwhelming the opponent and securing victory through sheer power and versatility.

The deck focuses on utilizing the combination of Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker to generate value and control the board. It leverages cards like Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble to disrupt the opponent's strategy while using Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End to finish the game. The deck also includes various utility lands like Grove of the Burnwillows and Eldrazi Temple to support its game plan. Additionally, it utilizes cards like Writhing Chrysalis and Blade of the Bloodchief to further enhance its combo potential and keep the pressure on the opponent.

The deck focuses on aggressively attacking the opponent while utilizing blink effects to gain value from creatures entering and leaving the battlefield. Cards like Flickerwisp and Ephemerate allow for repeatable triggers of powerful abilities, while creatures like Witch Enchanter and Quantum Riddler benefit from these triggers. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help to control the opponent's board, while powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Overlord of the Balemurk can quickly close out the game. Teferi, Time Raveler offers disruption and protection, allowing for a smooth gameplay experience.

The deck focuses on combining aggressive creature plays with blinking effects to gain value and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck can repeatedly flicker creatures like Solitude and Quantum Riddler to remove threats and draw cards. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Thoughtseize and removal spells like Fatal Push and Prismatic Ending. Overall, the strategy revolves around creating a fast and disruptive board presence while maintaining card advantage through blinking effects.
